title: Stichorkis rhombea
taxonomy: Stichorkis rhombea is classified in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Stichorkis and the subtribe Malaxideae. This orchid species is further grouped under the family Orchidaceae and the order Asparagales. The plant's classification also places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. Overall, its classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, subtribe Malaxideae, and genus Stichorkis.

intro: Stichorkis rhombea is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its unique, rhomboid-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Stichorkis rhombea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
